ops.title: Pack-shipping plugins use attach-only contract with captain-hook
ops.body:
# Attach-only pack contract (capt-hook >= 9.9.0)

A Claude plugin that ships a capt-hook pack (cc-context's `ccx`, cc-present) attaches it per session and never dispatches. The captain-hook plugin is the sole dispatcher — a pack-shipping plugin never wires a `capt-hook run` entry. The byte-identical-dedup doctrine (mirroring the dispatcher's `run <Event>` commands and relying on Claude Code's per-source dedup) is retired.

## Exactly four artifacts

1. `capt-hook.toml` — the pack manifest.
2. `hooks.json` — a single capt-hook entry: SessionStart running `uvx --isolated capt-hook pack attach "${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}"` (append `/hooks` when the manifest lives in a `hooks/` subtree). Zero `capt-hook run` entries, ever.
3. `plugin.json` — `"dependencies": [{ "name": "captain-hook", "marketplace": "captain-hook", "version": ">=9.9.0" }]` (object form with marketplace and version floor; a bare string fails lint).
4. The consumer marketplace.json — `"allowCrossMarketplaceDependenciesOn": ["captain-hook"]`. **Load-bearing**: without it Claude Code silently skips the declared dependency at install (no error — verified on Claude Code 2.1.207) and the attached pack runs with no dispatcher.

## CI check

`uvx capt-hook pack lint <plugin-root>` — one line; vets all four artifacts plus the pack, and fails a pack that subscribes `SessionStart` (attach and dispatch are unordered sibling hooks, so a pack's own SessionStart hooks are unreliable by construction).

## Install / upgrade story

Dependency auto-resolution requires the captain-hook marketplace added before install: `claude plugin marketplace add yasyf/captain-hook`, then `claude plugin install <plugin>@<marketplace>`. `claude plugin update` silently skips a dependency a new release adds — upgrading such a plugin means marketplace add + re-run `claude plugin install`.

## Unchanged

The plugin-canonical model for repo-local packs is untouched: `.claude/settings.json` stays hooks-free, `packs.toml` is the per-repo control surface, the captain-hook plugin registers all 12 events. A repo-scoped `packs.toml` pin beats an ambient plugin attach of the same-name pack, so the two never double-fire.

Documented in `plugins/repo-bootstrap/skills/repo-bootstrap/reference/hooks.md` (the `ccx` inventory entry); full authoring guide at https://yasyf.github.io/captain-hook/docs/guide/plugin-packs.html (source: captain-hook `docs/guide/plugin-packs.qmd`).
ops.when: Before modifying capt-hook integration or creating new pack-shipping plugins
